content: Glenn Fiedler's 'Fix Your Timestep!' accumulator pattern is the standard for decoupling fixed-rate simulation updates from variable-rate rendering: each frame, add elapsed wall-clock time to an accumulator; while accumulator >= fixed dt, run one sim step and subtract dt; render using remaining accumulator fraction to interpolate/extrapolate visual state between the last two sim states, avoiding both spiral-of-death (clamp max frame time) and jitter. For a 1-4Hz aircraft sim tick with 60fps rendering, interpolating aircraft position between the last two sim ticks each render frame gives smooth motion despite the low tick rate.
